content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 16, 1992


Frederick K. Lowell
Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 335
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-92-475

Dear Mr. Lowell:

This is to confirm advice given to you on September 4, 1992
pursuant to the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

You asked whether your client, the Committee on Jobs
("JOBS"), by mailing its quarterly newsletter, would be making a
contribution, independent expenditure or expenditure under the
Act.

JOBS is a non-profit corporation established to promote a
healthy business climate in San Francisco.  Its membership
consists of major corporations in the San Francisco area.  JOBS
does not sponsor political committees or make political
contributions.  The JOBS quarterly newsletter deals with issues
relating to the economic vitality of San Francisco.

In the fourth edition of its newsletter, JOBS intends to
include election-related material concerning the positions of
various persons running for public office.  The articles will
express agreement or disagreement with these positions, though the
format and length of the newsletter will be the same as the three
previous issues.  The newsletter will be distributed to the
corporate members of JOBS which, in turn, will distribute copies
to their employees.  Copies will also be distributed to persons
who request them.  Previous issues of the newsletter were
distributed to these persons, as well as to a limited number of
persons who did not request the publication.  The edition of the


1    Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  ●  P.O. Box 807  ●  Sacramento, CA  95812  ●  (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-475
Page 2


newsletter in question will not be distributed to this latter
group.

     As I advised you, based on these facts, the cost of producing
and distributing this newsletter would not be a contribution,
independent expenditure or expenditure under the Act.  We have
previously advised that the cost of producing and distributing
political communications that are included as part of an
organization's regularly published newsletter, which is sent only
to the organization's members, employees, shareholders,
individuals affiliated with the organization, or persons who
request the newsletter, are not contributions, independent
expenditures or expenditures.  (See Regulation 18225(b)(4)(c);
Lowell Advice Letter No. A-90-581.)  Those receiving the JOBS
newsletter fall within the group of persons to whom this exception
applies.

     Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916)
972-1420.2

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

SH:aa




2    Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question: You asked whether your client, the Committee on Jobs ("JOBS"), by mailing its quarterly newsletter, would be making a contribution, independent expenditure or expenditure under the Act.
sections.conclusion: based on these facts, the cost of producing and distributing this newsletter would not be a contribution, independent expenditure or expenditure under the Act. We have previously advised that the cost of producing and distributing political communications that are included as part of an organization's regularly published newsletter, which is sent only to the organization's members, employees, shareholders, individuals affiliated with the organization, or persons who request the newsletter, are not contributions, independent expenditures or expenditures.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether JOBS' distribution of its quarterly newsletter containing election-related material expressing positions on candidates constitutes a contribution, independent expenditure, or expenditure under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The cost of producing and distributing JOBS' newsletter does not constitute a contribution, independent expenditure, or expenditure under the Act because it is a regularly published newsletter distributed only to members, employees, affiliated individuals, and those who request it, falling within established regulatory exceptions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document confirms prior verbal advice given September 4, 1992. References Regulation 18225(b)(4)(c) and prior Lowell advice letter A-90-581.

QUESTION: You asked whether your client, the Committee on Jobs ("JOBS"), by mailing its quarterly newsletter, would be making a contribution, independent expenditure or expenditure under the Act.

CONCLUSION: based on these facts, the cost of producing and distributing this newsletter would not be a contribution, independent expenditure or expenditure under the Act. We have previously advised that the cost of producing and distributing political communications that are included as part of an organization's regularly published newsletter, which is sent only to the organization's members, employees, shareholders, individuals affiliated with the organization, or persons who request the newsletter, are not contributions, independent expenditures or expenditures.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that a non-profit organization's distribution of its quarterly newsletter containing election-related material expressing candidate positions does not constitute a contribution or expenditure under the Political Reform Act when distributed to members, employees, and requesters only.
